POS OF REPLY TO PLTF'S RESPONSE TO DEFT'S MTN FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FILED BY DEFENDANT CLEAR CHANNEL BROADCASTING, INC. CLEAR CHANNEL OUTDOOR, INC. CLEAR CHANNEL MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ERON BLOCK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. FIRST, PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CCP 437C OR CRC 3.1350. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT CITE ANY EVIDENCE. SECOND, PLAINTIFF REFERS TO DEFENDANT'S CLEAR CHANNEL OUTDOOR INC.'S ("CCO") RESPONSE TO THE REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS, BUT DOES NOT PROVIDE THE ACTUAL REQUEST. THIRD, DEFENDANT CCO DID NOT ACTUALLY ADMIT THAT THERE IS AN ENCROACHMENT. DEFENDANT DENIED THE REQUEST AND EXPLAINED THAT THERE APPEARED TO BE AN ENCROACHMENT. FOURTH, THE MOTION VIOLATES CCP 437C(F)(1) BECAUSE PLAINTIFF DOES NOT SEEK TO DISPOSE COMPLETELY OF THE ENTIRETY OF THESE TWO CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE REGARDING DAMAGES OR ENTITLEMENT TO AN INJUNCTION OR ANY OTHER RELIEF. FIFTH, DEFENDANT CORRECTLY CITES KAFKA V. BOZIO (1923) 191 CAL. 746, 750 AND PAHL V. RIBERO (1961) 193 CAL.APP.2ND 154, 163L. AN AIRSPACE ENCROACHMENT IS A NUISANCE, NOT A TRESPASS. THE COURT WILL SIGN ORDER SUBMITTED BY PREVALING PARTY. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CLEAR CHANNEL BROADCASTING, INC. AND CLEAR CHANNEL MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED AS TO CLEAR CHANNEL MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC. AND CLEAR CHANNEL BROADCASTING, INC. MOVING PARTIES HAVE SHOWN THAT THEY NEVER CONSTRUCTED, OWNED, OPERATED, MAINTAINED OR HAND ANY INVOLVEMENT IN ANY BILLBOARD IN THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, AND THAT THEY NEVER HAD ANY INVOLVEMENT WITH THE BILLBOARD WHICH ALLEGEDLY ENCROACHES ONTO PROPERTY DESCRIBED AS 1464 KANSAS STREET, SAN FRANCISCO, CA, AS ALLEGED IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE BURDEN SHIFTED TO PLAINTIFF. NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED, AND THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE COURT WILL SIGN ORDER SUBMITTED BY THE PREVAILING PARTY. calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-31-2013 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-28-2013 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-06-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: DENNIS VIGLIONE, ESQ., 916.929.5018, FOR DEFENDANT CLEAR CHANNEL OUTDOOR, INC.; No other appearances.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF ERON BLOCK's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. First, Plaintiff's separate statement does not comply with CCP 437c or CRC 3.1350. Plaintiff does not cite any evidence. Second, Plaintiff refers to Defendant's Clear Channel Outdoor Inc.'s ("CCO") response to the request for admissions, but does not provide the actual request. Third, Defendant CCO did not actually admit that there is an encroachment. Defendant denied the request and explained that there appeared to be an encroachment. Fourth, the motion violates CCP 437c(f)(1) because Plaintiff does not seek to dispose completely of the entirety of these two causes of action. Plaintiff presents no evidence regarding damages or entitlement to an injunction or any other relief. Fifth, Defendant correctly cites Kafka v. Bozio (1923) 191 Cal. 746, 750 and Pahl v. Ribero (1961) 193 Cal.App.2nd 154, 163l. An airspace encroachment is a nuisance, not a trespass. THE COURT WILL SIGN ORDER SUBMITTED BY PREVALING PARTY. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: DENNIS VIGLIONE, ESQ., 916.929.5018, FOR DEFENDANT CLEAR CHANNEL OUTDOOR, INC.; No other appearances.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CLEAR CHANNEL BROADCASTING, INC. and CLEAR CHANNEL MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted as to Clear Channel Management Services, Inc. and Clear Channel Broadcasting, Inc. Moving parties have shown that they never constructed, owned, operated, maintained or hand any involvement in any billboard in the state of California, and that they never had any involvement with the billboard which allegedly encroaches onto property described as 1464 Kansas Street, San Francisco, CA, as alleged in the First Amended Complaint. The burden shifted to Plaintiff. No opposition was filed, and the motion for summary judgment is granted. THE COURT WILL SIGN ORDER SUBMITTED BY THE PREVAILING PARTY. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
